item.title: Malaysia Holds Rate as Ringgit Tempers Inflation Pressure
item.description: Malaysia held its benchmark interest rate steady for a fifth straight meeting, despite heightened risks to growth from the prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: EU Startup Fund Makes First Bet With $160 Million Round in UK’s Quantum Motion
item.description: Kembara, a technology growth fund backed by the European Union, made its first investment, supporting a British startup that builds quantum computers running on silicon chips.

item.title: Brookfield Bets on Dubai Real Estate, Defying War Concerns
item.description: Private equity giant Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. is setting up a new property joint venture in Dubai with Gulf consumer giant Alshaya Group, a bold bet on the city’s real estate market even as the Iran war enters its third month.

item.title: Shell Profit Rises as Iran War Boosts Oil Price and Trading
item.description: Shell Plc said its profit surged in the first quarter as the Iran war drove oil and gas prices higher and the conflict caused a increase in volatility that boosted its big trading business.

item.title: AirAsia to Start New Airline, Stays Defiant on Fuel Hedging
item.description: AirAsia X Bhd. co-founder Tony Fernandes said he’s preparing to launch a new airline, betting that expanding while the aviation industry’s being roiled by high oil prices will pay off in the future.

item.title: Private Markets Could Support Europe’s Financing Push, ECB Says
item.description: The European Central Bank said burgeoning private markets could support a push by politicians in the region to channel savings into investments needed to modernize the economy.

item.title: FTSE 100 Poised to Rise on Iran Deal Optimism
Asian Bond Issuers Rush to Market as Iran Hopes Tighten Spreads
item.description: Asia’s dollar bond market has seen a burst of issuance as companies and governments rushed to secure cheaper funding, with credit spreads tightening to record lows on hopes tensions in the Middle East may ease.

item.title: Metals Rally Stalls as Traders Await Iran Reply to US Proposal
item.description: Copper edged lower with aluminum while Iran evaluates a fresh proposal from US President Donald Trump to end a conflict that’s nearing the 10-week mark.

item.title: Vodacom Kenya Unit Posts Record Profit, Cuts Ethiopia Losses
item.description: East African telecommunications operator Safaricom Plc’s annual profit jumped 67%, beating analysts’ expectations, after its unit in populous Ethiopia narrowed its loss.

item.title: Intrum to Raise $812 Million of Equity Capital to Trim Debt
item.description: Swedish credit management firm Intrum AB plans to raise 7.5 billion Swedish kronor ($812 million) of equity capital, as the company continues to struggle with a heavy debt load.

item.title: Europe’s Worst Earnings Are Coming From Luxury and Auto Sectors
item.description: Europe’s luxury firms, carmakers and hotels largely disappointed investors this earnings season, making consumer discretionary the worst-performing sector as rising inflation and geopolitical uncertainty derail a long-awaited recovery.

item.title: Inflation Fears Are Making Linkers a Winning Bond Trade in 2026
item.description: The last time oil prices spiked due to war in 2022, the rush to get protection with inflation-linked bonds turned out badly. So far, this time is proving different.

item.title: Humanoid Robots to Drive Next Leg of China Export Dominance
item.description: China’s early lead in humanoid robots will help power the next phase of its global manufacturing and export dominance, according to new research from Morgan Stanley.

item.title: Czechs Look Past Energy Shock for Now With Rates Set to Stay Put
item.description: Czech policymakers are likely to keep interest rates unchanged, taking more time to gauge the broader impact of more expensive fuels on living costs and economic growth.

item.title: Pan-African Infrastructure Fund Eyes Final Close at $400 Million
item.description: An African Development Bank private equity fund plans a fourth and final fund raising in June, as more financiers allocate capital to infrastructure projects on the continent.

item.title: Andreessen Horowitz in Funding Round for Swedish AI Startup Pit
item.description: Andreessen Horowitz has led a $16 million funding round for Pit, an artificial intelligence startup based in Sweden.

item.title: Goldman’s Singapore Co-Head and Key Engineering Exec Leaves Firm
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s co-head of Singapore and a key engineering executive is leaving the firm, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: EU Fails to Finalize US Trade Deal Despite Trump’s Demands
item.description: The European Union failed to finalize a long-delayed US trade deal during overnight talks, despite warnings from President Donald Trump that he would soon impose fresh tariffs.

item.title: Philippine Peso Set for Best Daily Gain in a Month on Oil Relief
item.description: The Philippine peso is headed for its best gain in a month as optimism over a US-Iran peace deal boosts prospects for further declines in oil prices.

item.title: Yen Intervention Rally Faces High Hurdle at 155 to the Dollar
item.description: The yen’s failure to breach 155 to the dollar after repeated cases of suspected intervention by Japan is raising questions over the durability of the currency’s recent advance.

item.title: OceanaGold Hunts for More Deals to Add to Strong Growth Pipeline
item.description: Canadian gold miner OceanaGold Corp. is aggressively canvassing options to buy other producers and existing operations while also pursuing a strong organic growth strategy, with two new mines in development.

item.title: Skyroot Rockets to Unicorn Status Backed by GIC, BlackRock Funds
item.description: Skyroot Aerospace Pvt. has become India’s first space-tech unicorn after securing $60 million in fresh funding from GIC Pte and Sherpalo Ventures, lifting its valuation to $1.1 billion.

item.title: Mbappé Will Power France to World Cup Gold Over Spain, BofA Says
item.description: France will win this year’s FIFA World Cup and claim its third title by beating Spain in the final, Bank of America Corp. analysts predicted.

item.title: European Industry Is in Crosshairs of China’s New Five-Year Plan
item.description: For Europe, a new cycle of Chinese rivalry heralded by its latest roadmap for advancement is forcing soul searching on how to respond.

item.title: Oil and Stocks Depend on How Serious Trump Deal Is
item.description: Markets care far more about the Strait than Iran’s nuclear program.

item.title: Africa’s Newest Oil Refinery Fires Up to Supply Fuel for Angola
item.description: Angola’s Cabinda oil refinery, among the first to be built in Africa in decades, has started shipping fuel, helping the crude producer bolster domestic supply and reduce reliance on imports.

item.title: The Bank of England Joins Those Who Doubt UK Growth Figures
item.description: Figures next week are likely to paint a rosy picture for the UK economy in the first quarter. The trouble is that fewer and fewer people believe them.

item.title: Alibaba Shares Outpace Tencent’s as Chip Exposure Fuels Demand
item.description: The torrid stock rally in Asian chipmakers is driving a divergence between China’s two internet giants, with Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. gaining an edge over rival Tencent Holdings Ltd. due to investor enthusiasm about its ambitious semiconductor unit.

item.title: AI Boom Trumps Sleep, Says Boss of Data Center Operator NEXTDC
item.description: Short on sleep but flush with new funds, the head of Australian data center operator NEXTDC Ltd. has a message to investors: You snooze, you lose.
